hero.headline: When someone else has to step in, they shouldn't have to start from zero.
hero.lede: Everything about caring for someone — their medications, doctors, conditions, and daily needs — in one private place any family member can pick up. Offline, printable.
hero.note: Instant download · No subscription · No account · Works on Mac, Windows, tablets & phones
hero.imageAlt: Caregiving Companion showing the Medications register

intro.heading: A parent's care, gathered in one place
intro.body: Everything about caring for someone — their medications, their doctors, their conditions, and how they like their day — kept in one private place any family member can pick up.

screens.caption: Doctors, pharmacy, home health, and family.
What a new caregiver or hospital needs to know.

privacy.heading: Private by design, not by promise
privacy.body: Nothing you type ever leaves your device, and it never asks for an insurance, Medicare, or account number by design.

cards.title: Zero network requests
cards.body: The app is a single file that runs entirely in your browser, on your device. It makes no connections and loads nothing from the internet. You can run it with Wi-Fi off.

cards.title: Never a password
cards.body: By design, it never asks for or stores passwords or PINs. It records that accounts exist and where your written instructions live — the map, never the keys.

cards.title: Yours forever
cards.body: One-time purchase. The file is yours, works offline for life, and will still open years from now, exactly as it does today.

priceCard.sub: the one place a parent's whole care picture lives — medications, doctors, conditions, and wishes — so any family member or a fill-in caregiver can step in, and an ER visit isn't a scramble.
bullets: 28 guided tasks across four phases: Get the Picture, Build the Team, Run the Day-to-Day, Plan Ahead & Hand Off
A medications register — dose, schedule, prescriber, and purpose for each one
A care team directory: doctors, specialists, pharmacy, home health, and the family who help
Health facts an ER or new provider needs, and where the insurance card and advance directive live
A care wishes section: daily routine, food, comfort, and what matters to them
A one-click printable Care Summary — 'What a New Caregiver or ER Needs to Know'

faq.a: No. You pay 39 once and the app is yours forever. There is no account, no renewal, and nothing that expires.

faq.q: Do I need to install anything?
faq.a: No. You download a small file, double-click it, and it opens in the browser you already use — on Mac, Windows, tablets, and phones.
Yes, structurally. The app makes zero network requests — you can verify this with Wi-Fi turned off. Your entries live only on your device, and your backup is a file you control.

faq.q: What if it doesn't work for me?
faq.a: Email hello@quietkeep.co and we'll make it right. We answer every message.

crossSell.heading: Thinking further ahead, too?
crossSell.body: Fifth Quietkeep organizer; the usage-life companion to the estate set — families caring for a parent are often the same ones who will later settle the estate.
